properties.name: Kaiserpfalz Franconofurd
properties.description: The historical heart of early Frankfurt. Emperor Charlemagne's seat of power.

properties.name: Römerkastell Saalburg
properties.description: A completely reconstructed Roman cohort fort located on the Upper Germanic Limes.

properties.name: Kaiserpfalz Gelnhausen
properties.description: The best-preserved Palas architectural ruin built under Emperor Friedrich I Barbarossa.

properties.name: Staufenmauer
properties.description: The ancient stone fortifications built to protect the core boundaries of Alt-Frankfurt.

properties.name: Eschenheimer Turm
properties.description: The finest of Frankfurt's former 42 town wall watchtowers, standing 47 meters tall as a striking relic of the medieval city fortifications.

properties.name: Saalhof
properties.description: The oldest preserved structural monument in Frankfurt's historic center, originally built as a Staufer-era royal imperial castle (Reichsburg).

properties.name: Rententurm
properties.description: A fortified harbor gate tower along the Main River, serving as the strategic customs house and defensive anchor of the Saalhof complex.

properties.name: Kuhhirtenturm
properties.description: A southern bastion tower in Alt-Sachsenhausen that originally guarded the bridgeheads, later famously used as a residence by composer Paul Hindemith.

properties.name: Kaiserdom St. Bartholomäus
properties.description: The Imperial Cathedral of Saint Bartholomew. The grand site of election and coronation for the Holy Roman Emperors.

properties.name: Hauptwache
properties.description: The historic central guardhouse and prison facility of Frankfurt's municipal militia, famously stormed during the 1833 democratic uprising.

properties.name: Schloss Höchst
properties.description: The former residency of the Archbishops of Mainz, built to enforce tariffs and safeguard Mainz's territory on the western flank of Frankfurt.

properties.name: Staufenmauer (An der Staufenmauer)
properties.description: A surviving open-air stretch of Frankfurt's first massive stone ring-wall, built under the Hohenstaufen emperors to shield the expanding Altstadt.

properties.name: Der Römer
properties.description: The historic, three-gabled city hall complex of Frankfurt that hosted the coronation banquets of the Holy Roman Empire.

properties.name: Alte Stadtwaage
properties.description: The former municipal weighing house and central standard-measure office, a masterfully framed half-timbered house destroyed during WWII and rebuilt in the New Old Town.

properties.name: Friedberger Warte
properties.description: One of the original fortified outer watchtowers of Frankfurt's Landwehr defense system, strategically guarding the trade route to Friedberg.

properties.name: Sachsenhäuser Warte
properties.description: A fortified watchtower built as part of the outer defense ring to protect the southern flanks of Sachsenhausen along the trade route to Darmstadt.

properties.name: Bockenheimer Warte
properties.description: A landmark watchtower guarding the western border of Frankfurt's territory, surviving as a stark architectural anchor of the Bockenheim district.

properties.name: Galluswarte
properties.description: Originally known as the Galgenwarte, this outer defensive watchtower guarded the western trade routes through the Mainzer Landstraße.

properties.name: Burg Kronberg
properties.description: An impressive hilltop castle complex in the Taunus mountains that served as the ancestral seat of the powerful Knights of Kronberg.

properties.name: Burgruine Königstein
properties.description: One of the largest fortress ruins in Germany, which guarded highly strategic military and trade paths through the Taunus range before its destruction.

properties.name: Römerkastell Saalburg
properties.description: A highly fortified cohort fort along the Upper Germanic Limes, reconstructed in the late 19th century as an open-air archaeological park.

properties.name: St. Leonhard
properties.description: A Romanesque basilica originally gifted by Emperor Frederick II, located right next to the historic city wall line near the Main River.

properties.name: Karmeliterkloster
properties.description: The only preserved monastery complex in Frankfurt's Altstadt, hosting remarkable late medieval murals by Jörg Ratgeb and housing the Archaeological Museum.

properties.name: Alte Brücke
properties.description: Frankfurt's oldest stone bridge crossing, which historically featured defensive towers and gates to control passage across the Main River.

properties.name: Römerstadt Nida (Kastell Heddernheim)
properties.description: An ancient Roman military garrison fort and administrative capital of Civitas Taunensium, once standing as a massive settlement on the modern-day northern edge of Frankfurt.

properties.name: Altkönig Ringwall
properties.description: A prehistoric Celtic hillfort with two concentric monumental dry-stone ring walls, sitting atop the high peak of the Altkönig in the Taunus mountains.

properties.name: Einhard-Basilika (Seligenstadt)
properties.description: One of the absolute finest surviving examples of pure Carolingian architecture north of the Alps, erected by Einhard, the personal biographer of Charlemagne.

properties.name: Königshalle Lorsch
properties.description: The world-famous Torhalle (Gatehouse) of the former Lorsch Abbey. A UNESCO World Heritage site and a breathtaking monument to early medieval imperial representation.

properties.name: Heidenmauer (Frankfurt-Sossenheim)
properties.description: Remnants of ancient late-Roman boundary and defensive wall structures positioned along early trade corridors in the Nidda river valleys.

properties.name: Römerkastell Kesselstadt
properties.description: One of the largest Roman auxiliary forts built during the Flavian-era campaigns to control crossings along the northern banks of the Main River.

properties.name: Römerkastell Lochmühle
properties.description: A small fortified Roman border outpost (Kleinkastell) erected to garrison troops guarding the direct pass routes over the Limes boundary line.

properties.name: Heidetränk-Oppidum
properties.description: One of the largest Celtic city-complexes in Central Europe, enclosed by kilometers of massive defensive gravel-core and timber walls.

properties.name: Kastell Rheingönheim (Mainz-Kastel)
properties.description: The crucial early-Roman military bridgehead fortress (Castellum Mattiacorum) built directly opposite Mogontiacum to secure safe access to the interior.

properties.name: Alt-Sachsenhausen Merovingian Graves
properties.description: Archaeological site of an early Frankish burial ground, proving permanent medieval settlement of the riverbanks long before Frankfurt's first formal mention.
